ontology.foodon_gap_note: No specific FoodOn term exists for mango cream sponge cake; closest_parent 'cake food product' (FOODON_03301116) used as the nearest defensible ancestor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC (Cakes) is the closest parent category; no specific code exists for Asian-style mango cream sponge cake.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226529007 'Cake (food)' is the closest available parent concept; no specific mango cake concept exists.

serving.natural_language_portion: One slice of mango cake weighing approximately 120 g, representing a typical single-serve portion as sold by the slice at Sinpopo Coffee.

nutrient_source.title: Cake, sponge, with cream and fruit filling — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 18133 adapted); supplemented with USDA Foundation Foods data for fresh mango (FDC ID 169910) and whipped cream (FDC ID 170859)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact match for Asian-style mango cotton sponge cake exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA databases. Values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy 'Cake, sponge, with cream and fruit filling' (NDB 18133) as the closest defensible analogue, cross-referenced with USDA Foundation Foods data for fresh mango and whipped cream components. The cotton sponge style is lighter than a standard Western sponge; energy and fat values are adjusted downward by approximately 10% to reflect the lower-fat, lower-density Japanese-style cotton sponge base typical of this product category. All adaptations are documented in notes.

gi_source.title: Sponge cake, plain — Glycemic Index Database, University of Sydney; supplemented by Atkinson FS et al. (2021) International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values 2021, American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Asian-style mango cotton sponge cake. GI of 54 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for plain sponge cake (GI ~46–66 range; mean ~54 used) and cross-referenced with Atkinson et al. 2021 International Tables. The fresh mango component (GI ~51) and whipped cream (negligible GI contribution) do not substantially alter the overall GI. A composite GI of 54 is adopted as the best defensible estimate. GL per serving = (54 × 41.8 g carbohydrate per 120 g serving) / 100 = 22.6, rounded to 22.5. gi_category set to 'low' per the ≤55 threshold rule.

hypertension.key_factor: Moderate sodium content (~138 mg per slice) and high sugar load
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to half a slice, as the combined sodium (~138 mg) and high sugar content (~27 g per slice) can contribute to fluid retention and elevated blood pressure when consumed regularly.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High sugar content and significant glycemic load per serving (GL ~22.5)
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should treat this cake as an occasional indulgence only; the glycemic load of approximately 22.5 per slice and high sugar content (~27 g per slice) will produce a significant postprandial glucose rise and should be paired with physical activity or insulin adjustment as clinically appropriate.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (~7.3 g per slice) and cholesterol (~82 mg per slice) from cream and eggs
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption of this cake due to its saturated fat content (~7.3 g per slice) from whipped cream and eggs, which may adversely affect LDL cholesterol levels when consumed frequently.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Old Fashioned Mango Cake (Sliced) is an Asian-style celebration and café dessert cake sold by the slice at Sinpopo Coffee in Singapore. It is constructed from a cotton soft Japanese-style sponge cake base — a light, airy, and moist sponge achieved through a chiffon or cotton-cake technique that produces a finer, more delicate crumb than a traditional Western sponge. The sponge layers are filled and topped with fresh, ripe mango pieces and a generous layer of lightly sweetened whipped cream. The overall flavour profile is sweet, fruity, and milky, with the natural tropical tartness of fresh mango balancing the richness of the cream. The texture is simultaneously fluffy, creamy, and juicy. Nutritionally, one 120 g slice provides approximately 298 kcal, 41.8 g of carbohydrate (of which ~27 g is sugar), 12.6 g of fat (of which ~7.3 g is saturated), and 5.0 g of protein. The glycemic load per slice is approximately 22.5, placing it in the moderate-to-high concern category for blood glucose management. The cake is vegetarian and halal-suitable but contains gluten, dairy, and eggs. It is not appropriate for individuals with coeliac disease or dairy intolerance. For people with Type 2 Diabetes, the high sugar and glycemic load make this a high-concern food to be consumed only occasionally and in small portions. For cardiovascular health, the saturated fat from cream and eggs warrants moderation. The dish reflects the broader Singaporean café culture of adapting Japanese confectionery techniques to local tropical fruit ingredients, producing a distinctly Southeast Asian dessert experience.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a slice of mango cake?
Is mango cake suitable for diabetics?
What is the GI of mango sponge cake?
How much sugar is in a slice of mango cake?
What is old fashioned mango cake made of?
Asian mango cake vs Western cake nutrition

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional match for Asian-style mango cotton sponge cake exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Values are adapted from the closest USDA SR Legacy analogue (sponge cake with cream and fruit filling) with a documented downward adjustment for the lighter cotton sponge base. GI is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database sponge cake entry. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy NDB 18133 (Cake, sponge, with cream and fruit filling) with approximately 10% downward adjustment to energy and fat to reflect the lighter Japanese-style cotton sponge base. This adaptation is the best available defensible estimate in the absence of an exact Singapore-specific database entry. 2. vitamin_d_mcg is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source for this food category. selenium_mcg is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source for this food category. 3. GI of 54 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database sponge cake entry (mean of reported range ~46–66); gi_category is 'low' per the ≤55 threshold rule. 4. Serving size of 120 g is estimated based on typical single-slice portion for a whole cake priced at SGD 19.50, consistent with standard café slice portions in Singapore. 5. Halal status is assumed based on Sinpopo Coffee's general menu positioning; formal halal certification should be verified with the restaurant directly. 6. The food is sold as a whole cake sliced to order; the per-100g values represent the assembled product including sponge, mango, and cream layers in typical proportions.
